questions.title: Medication Calculation
questions.text: A patient is prescribed digoxin 0.125 mg daily. The medication is available in 0.25 mg tablets. How many tablets should the nurse administer?
questions.rationale: 0.125 mg / 0.25 mg/tablet = 0.5 tablets. The nurse would administer half a tablet.

questions.title: Infection Control
questions.text: Which of the following are standard precautions used to prevent the spread of infection? Select all that apply.
questions.rationale: Standard precautions apply to all patients regardless of their diagnosis. Airborne and contact precautions are used in addition to standard precautions for specific infections. Sterile technique is not always required.

questions.title: Cardiac Assessment
questions.text: A patient experiencing angina reports chest pain radiating to the jaw and left arm. What is the priority nursing intervention?

choices.text: Assess vital signs and oxygen saturation.
Instruct the patient to take deep breaths.

questions.rationale: While all options are appropriate, assessing vital signs and oxygen saturation is the immediate priority to determine the severity of the situation and guide further interventions.

questions.title: Respiratory Assessment
questions.text: A patient with chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD) is experiencing increased shortness of breath and wheezing. Which assessment finding would require immediate attention?

choices.text: Use of accessory muscles for breathing

questions.rationale: An oxygen saturation of 88% indicates hypoxemia, a life-threatening condition requiring immediate intervention. The other findings are concerning but not as immediately life-threatening.

questions.title: Diabetes Management
questions.text: A patient with type 1 diabetes is exhibiting signs of hypoglycemia. Which of the following are appropriate nursing interventions? Select all that apply.

choices.text: Offer the patient 15 grams of carbohydrates.
Check for ketones in the urine.

questions.rationale: Administering rapid-acting insulin would worsen hypoglycemia. Checking for ketones is relevant for hyperglycemia, not hypoglycemia.

questions.title: Fluid Volume Assessment
questions.text: A patient with severe dehydration would likely exhibit which of the following symptoms?
questions.rationale: Dehydration leads to decreased blood volume, resulting in hypotension. Bradycardia, edema, and bounding pulses are not associated with dehydration.

questions.title: Pain Management
questions.text: A patient reports a pain level of 8/10 after surgery. Which of the following is the most appropriate initial action?

choices.text: Reassess the patient's pain in 30 minutes.
Teach the patient about nonpharmacological pain management techniques.
Tell the patient to try to tough it out.

questions.rationale: Given the high pain level, administering the prescribed analgesic is the most appropriate initial action.

questions.title: Wound Care
questions.text: Which of the following is the best method for cleaning a wound?
questions.rationale: Sterile saline solution is the most appropriate solution for wound cleaning to minimize tissue damage and infection risk.

questions.title: Neurological Assessment
questions.text: A patient with a head injury is exhibiting signs of increased intracranial pressure (ICP). Which of the following are indicative of increased ICP? Select all that apply.
questions.rationale: Increased ICP typically presents with changes in level of consciousness, widening pulse pressure (Cushing's triad), irregular respirations (Cheyne-Stokes), and hypertension. Bradycardia and pinpoint pupils are not typical signs of increased ICP.

questions.title: Nasogastric Tube Management
questions.text: A patient with a nasogastric (NG) tube is experiencing nausea and vomiting. What is the priority nursing action?

choices.text: Increase the rate of NG tube feeding.
Check the placement of the NG tube.
Administer an antiemetic medication as ordered.
Encourage the patient to drink clear liquids.

questions.rationale: Checking NG tube placement is the priority to ensure that the feeding is not entering the lungs.

questions.title: Medication Administration
questions.text: Before administering any medication, the nurse should:

choices.text: Ask the patient their name and date of birth.
Verify the medication order with the MAR.

questions.rationale: All three actions are essential steps in medication administration to ensure patient safety.

questions.title: Oxygen Therapy
questions.text: A patient is receiving oxygen via a nasal cannula. What is the maximum flow rate typically used with a nasal cannula?
questions.rationale: Higher flow rates with nasal cannulas can cause nasal dryness and irritation, and may not increase oxygen saturation significantly.

questions.title: Postoperative Assessment
questions.text: A priority assessment for a postoperative patient is:
questions.rationale: All three are crucial for postoperative patient safety and recovery.

questions.title: Wound Management
questions.text: What is the best way to clean a new wound?

choices.text: Leave the wound uncovered to air dry.

questions.rationale: Soap and water are the best way to clean a new wound to minimize infection.

questions.title: Thyroid Assessment
questions.text: Signs and symptoms of hyperthyroidism include: Select all that apply.
questions.rationale: Hyperthyroidism causes increased metabolism, leading to weight loss, heat intolerance, and tremors. A goiter may also be present. Bradycardia and constipation are associated with hypothyroidism.

questions.title: Urinary Catheter Care
questions.text: A patient with a urinary catheter should have their intake and output monitored closely. Why is this important?
questions.rationale: Accurate I&O monitoring is crucial for all three reasons.

questions.title: Holistic Nursing Care
questions.text: A patient is experiencing significant anxiety related to a recent diagnosis. Which nursing intervention demonstrates a holistic approach to care?

choices.text: Facilitating communication with family and support systems.

questions.rationale: A holistic approach involves addressing the patient's physical, emotional, and social needs.

questions.title: Hypertension Management
questions.text: A patient has a blood pressure of 160/95 mmHg. What is the priority intervention?

choices.text: Reassess the blood pressure in 5 minutes.
Place the patient in a supine position.

questions.rationale: Reassessing the blood pressure to confirm the reading is the priority intervention before taking further action.

questions.title: Blood Transfusion
questions.text: Which of the following are appropriate nursing interventions during a blood transfusion? Select all that apply.

choices.text: Monitor vital signs every 15 minutes for the first hour.
Administer the blood with 5% dextrose solution.
Slow the infusion if the patient develops a fever.

questions.rationale: Blood should never be administered with dextrose solutions as they can cause hemolysis of red blood cells.

questions.title: Seizure Management
questions.text: During a seizure, the priority nursing action is to:

choices.text: Place something in the patient's mouth.
Ensure patient safety by removing nearby objects.

questions.rationale: The priority during a seizure is to protect the patient from injury by removing nearby objects that could cause harm.

questions.title: Diabetes Management
questions.text: A client with type 2 diabetes has a blood glucose level of 45 mg/dL and is confused. Which intervention should the nurse implement first?

choices.text: Give orange juice with added sugar

questions.rationale: For conscious clients with hypoglycemia, oral glucose is the first intervention. Orange juice with sugar provides fast-acting carbohydrates to quickly raise blood glucose levels.

questions.title: Postpartum Assessment
questions.text: A nurse is assessing a client 4 hours after vaginal delivery. Which findings should the nurse report to the healthcare provider? Select all that apply.

choices.text: Urinary output of 100 mL in 4 hours

questions.rationale: Decreased urinary output, elevated temperature, and elevated blood pressure require immediate provider notification as they may indicate complications like infection or preeclampsia. Lochia rubra with small clots and a firm, midline fundus are normal findings in the immediate postpartum period.

questions.title: Medication Calculation
questions.text: The healthcare provider orders Heparin 1,000 units/hour IV. The label reads 25,000 units/500 mL. Calculate the mL/hour to be administered.
questions.rationale: Using dimensional analysis: (1,000 units/hour × 500 mL) ÷ 25,000 units = 20 mL/hour

questions.title: Respiratory Assessment
questions.text: A client has crackles in the lung bases. Which position should the nurse place the client in to facilitate breathing?
questions.rationale: High Fowler's position (sitting upright at a 60-90 degree angle) allows for maximum lung expansion, especially in the bases, which helps mobilize secretions and improve ventilation in clients with crackles.

questions.title: Pain Management in Post-Op
questions.text: A postoperative client reports pain of 7/10 despite receiving the prescribed opioid analgesic 2 hours ago. Which nursing interventions are appropriate? Select all that apply.

choices.text: Administer the prescribed PRN opioid immediately
Assess the client's pain using a comprehensive pain assessment tool
Encourage the client to wait until the next scheduled dose

questions.rationale: Appropriate interventions include notifying the provider about inadequate pain control, performing a comprehensive pain assessment, and offering non-pharmacological measures. The nurse should not administer another dose if it's too early per orders, and should not encourage the client to simply endure uncontrolled pain.
